message:
fix(runtime): detect no-progress for tasks that never checkpoint via logical time

The deadline monitor previously relied solely on wall-clock comparison
(now_instant.duration_since(entry.last_progress)) to detect stalled
tasks.  This missed a category of stuck tasks: those that were created
long before the monitor's first scan and have never called checkpoint().

For such tasks, entry.last_progress is initialized to the scan instant
(not the task creation time), so the wall-clock delta starts at zero on
first scan and grows slowly — the task could be logically stalled for
minutes before the checkpoint_timeout triggers.

Added logical_no_progress: when a task has never been checkpointed
(last_checkpoint_seen is None) AND its logical age (now - created_at)
exceeds the checkpoint_timeout, the monitor now flags it immediately on
first scan rather than waiting for the wall-clock timeout to accumulate
from the first observation.

The two paths are ORed: no_progress = wall_no_progress || logical_no_progress.
Wall-clock detection continues to handle tasks that checkpoint initially
but then stop, while logical detection handles the "never started" case.

Also fixed the warns_on_no_progress test: was passing None for
last_progress (simulating a task never observed), which would cause
duration_since to compute relative to epoch.  Now passes
Some(Instant::now()) to correctly model a task that was observed but
stopped making progress.

Added test: warns_on_no_progress_for_old_task_without_checkpoint_on_first_scan
— verifies that a task created at t=0 with no checkpoints and scanned at
t=100s is immediately flagged as NoProgress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
